claudeCode.summary: The productivity debate is peaking — HN threads from 60-year-olds rediscovering passion to 'Great Productivity Panic' essays. Meanwhile, the 1M context window has a documented reliability gap (works to ~256K, degrades after), and rate limits remain the #1 daily friction. The real product opportunity: persistent task management across sessions doesn't exist yet.

items.title: 1M context window doesn't deliver what's advertised
items.snippet: Bug report with measurable data: 1M-token context advertised, but reliable performance degrades past ~200-256K tokens. Gap between marketing and product. Opportunity: tiered context strategies — smart chunking and retrieval rather than brute-force window size.

items.title: Persistent task management is a missing product layer
items.snippet: TodoWrite disappears when sessions end. No persistent backlog, no cross-session continuity. Users build workarounds with files and scripts. Opportunity: a durable task layer that survives sessions would unlock true multi-day agentic workflows.

items.title: The Great Productivity Panic — metrics vs meaning debate
items.snippet: Are 10x productivity claims real or are developers measuring wrong? Mental exhaustion after 3-hour agentic sessions vs 10-hour flow states. Opportunity: developer wellbeing metrics — session length, cognitive load, intervention frequency — as product signals.

items.title: Rate limits are the daily ceiling nobody planned for
items.snippet: Claude Code and claude.ai share the same token pool. Afternoon coding shrinks after morning chat sessions. $200/month may cost $5K in compute. Opportunity: usage-aware rate allocation — separate pools, predictive budgeting, or cost visibility per session.

items.title: claude:ignore directive — granular context control still missing
items.snippet: Feature request for inline file-level ignore directives. .claudeignore exists but is coarse-grained. Users need per-file, per-section control over what enters context. Opportunity: a context budget UI that shows what's loaded, what's costing tokens, and lets you prune.

openClaw.summary: Security is now an existential crisis — 'security nightmare' is trending on HN with 30+ CVEs in 2026 alone and 800+ malicious skills found in ClawHub. The v2026.3.22 release drops 30 security patches and introduces ClawHub as the default store, but trust is bleeding faster than patches can fix. Meanwhile, the architecture is being commoditised (10MB Go alternatives) — the moat is the ecosystem, not the code.

items.title: Security nightmare narrative is becoming consensus
items.snippet: Two HN front-page posts in 24 hours: 'security nightmare' + 'is it really?' — 30+ CVEs this year including RCE, SSRF, privilege escalation. 40K exposed instances. 800+ malicious skills. Opportunity: security-as-a-service layer for OpenClaw — scan, sandbox, and certify skills before install.

items.title: ClawHub is live but has no trust signals
items.snippet: v2026.3.22 makes ClawHub the default plugin store. But no ratings, no verified publishers, no security badges. 7.1% of existing skills expose credentials. Opportunity: a curated, audited skills marketplace with trust tiers would own the distribution layer.

items.title: ContextEngine plugin API opens the memory wars
items.snippet: New ContextEngine slot with full lifecycle hooks lets plugins replace core context management. Memory typing and structured extraction being designed in the open. Opportunity: whoever solves persistent, accurate agent memory across sessions wins the long-running agent use case.

items.title: OpenAI acquisition of founder signals platform risk
items.snippet: Peter Steinberger joined OpenAI, project moved to a foundation. Community debating governance and long-term direction. Opportunity: governance uncertainty creates a window for forks or competitors to capture developers who want stability.

items.title: Lightweight alternatives commoditising the runtime
items.snippet: Capabot (10MB Go) and NemoClaw (NVIDIA) both running OpenClaw-compatible skills. The agent runtime is being commoditised. Opportunity: value is shifting from runtime to ecosystem — skills marketplace, integrations catalogue, and enterprise trust layer are the real moats.
